MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jan-15-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
David J. Kestenbaum of LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, (415-362-2580) for defendant / moving party The W.W. Henry Company
David Amell of MAUNE; RAICHLE; HARTLEY, FRENCH & MUDD, LLC (800-358-5922), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Donald Keith Anders

RULING:
DEFENDANT THE W.W. HENRY COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant The W.W. Henry Company's motion for summary adjudication is denied. As to plaintiff's claim for punitive damages, defendant sustained its initial burden. The deposition testimony of Lori Angelo and Paul Beemer and defendant's responses to standard interrogatories create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. As to plaintiff's cause of action for false representation, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting this cause of action. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because it did not demonstrate that plaintiff failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive written discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support his false representation cause of action. An order after hearing and an order re: defendant's objections to plaintiff's evidence offered in opposition of motion were signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Angela Sinclair of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 13902 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Off Calendar Jan-08-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Laurel Halbany of MAUNE; RAICHLE; HARTLEY, FRENCH & MUDD, LLC (800-358-5922), appearing for plaintiff / moving party Donald Keith Anders
Brian H. Buddell of POND NORTH LLP (415-217-1240), appearing for defendant / opposing party Sears Roebuck & Co.
Christopher D. Strunk of GORDON & REES LLP (415-986-5900), appearing for defendant / opposing party Mannington Mills, Inc.
(Refer to sign-in sheet for appearances on behalf of defendants B.R. Funsten & Co., Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc., Butler-Johnson Corporation, Domco Products Texas Inc., and The W.W. Henry Company)

RULING:
PLAINTIFF DONALD ANDERS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION RE: DUTY OF SHIPYARD OWNER

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion for summary adjudication re: duty of shipyard owner is denied. The Court cannot summarily adjudicate the issue of whether a non-defendant did or did not owe a duty to a plaintiff. (C.C.P. 437c(f)(1).) However, even if the Court could, the motion would be denied because plaintiff failed to sustain his initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff failed to sustain his burden of demonstrating that defendants do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that the shipyard owner did owe a duty to plaintiff. Plaintiff failed to sustain his burden of submitting affirmative evidence to demonstrate that the shipyard owner did not owe him a duty and thus cannot sustain his burden under the negation of an element prong. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Claudia J. Knap of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #6099 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR A PREFERENTIAL TRIAL DATE
Hearing held. The Court made the following ruling: Plaintiffs' motion for a preferential trial date is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a) & (d). Trial is January 20, 2015 at 11:15am in Department 503. The 120th day is January 21, 2015. Last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is January 15, 2015. All parties stipulate that summary judgment/adjudication motions may be brought on 28 days' notice, with oppositions due 13 days before the hearings, and replies due 7 days. Time to respond to written discovery is shortened to 20 days. E-service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Plaintiff's deposition must conclude by Oct. 24, 2014. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. The meet and confer should happen 10 days before filing a motion.. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than November 12, 2014. Responses to the demand are due no later than November 19, 2014. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by January 12, 2015 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 1:30pm on January 14, 2015. Fact discovery cut-off date is January 9, 2015. Expert discovery cut-off date is January 16, 2015. No later than October 21, 2014 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than October 21, 2014 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on October 29, 2014 at 1:30pm. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, plaintiff shall notify the Court at least two days prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on January 20, 2015 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by January 16, 2015 at 2:00p.m. No need to attach exhibits to the statements. Clients must be present at the conference except on order of the order. The parties put their stipulated terms on the record including (1) the social security authorization must be provided to the designated defense counsel by October 6, 2014, (2) expert disclosure is 45 days before trial, (3) plaintiff will provide information to all defendants within 3 court days of any submission of bankruptcy trust claim. Refer to reporter's transcript for all stipulated terms. The Court's 5/7/14 Trial Management Order remains in effect except the specific terms stipulated by the parties. Plaintiffs to prepare a proposed order and forward to Berry & Berry to be conformed before submitting to the Court for signature. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Erin F.
